title: MySQL Processlist & Performance
description: Live active queries, top queries by time, active transactions, lock waits — direct SQL against performance_schema

panels.title: Active Queries — SHOW PROCESSLIST (non-sleeping)
panels.description: Live view of all running queries. Refresh every 5s. Long-running queries here are candidates for KILL.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  PROCESSLIST_ID          AS id,
  PROCESSLIST_USER        AS user,
  PROCESSLIST_HOST        AS host,
  PROCESSLIST_DB          AS db,
  PROCESSLIST_COMMAND     AS command,
  PROCESSLIST_TIME        AS time_sec,
  PROCESSLIST_STATE       AS state,
  LEFT(IFNULL(PROCESSLIST_INFO, ''), 250) AS query
FROM performance_schema.processlist
WHERE PROCESSLIST_COMMAND != 'Sleep'
ORDER BY PROCESSLIST_TIME DESC

panels.title: Top 20 Queries by Total Time — performance_schema.events_statements_summary_by_digest
panels.description: Aggregate query patterns ranked by cumulative execution time since last TRUNCATE or restart. These are your tuning targets.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  SCHEMA_NAME                                          AS db,
  SUBSTRING(REPLACE(REPLACE(DIGEST_TEXT, '\n', ' '), '  ', ' '), 1, 120) AS query_pattern,
  COUNT_STAR                                           AS calls,
  ROUND(AVG_TIMER_WAIT      / 1e9, 2)                 AS avg_ms,
  ROUND(MAX_TIMER_WAIT      / 1e9, 2)                 AS max_ms,
  ROUND(SUM_TIMER_WAIT      / 1e9, 2)                 AS total_ms,
  ROUND(SUM_ROWS_EXAMINED   / COUNT_STAR)             AS avg_rows_examined,
  ROUND(SUM_ROWS_SENT       / COUNT_STAR)             AS avg_rows_sent,
  SUM_NO_INDEX_USED                                    AS no_index_used,
  SUM_NO_GOOD_INDEX_USED                               AS bad_index_used
FROM performance_schema.events_statements_summary_by_digest
WHERE DIGEST_TEXT IS NOT NULL
  AND SCHEMA_NAME NOT IN ('mysql', 'performance_schema', 'information_schema', 'sys')
ORDER BY SUM_TIMER_WAIT DESC
LIMIT 20

panels.title: Active InnoDB Transactions
panels.description: Long-running transactions hold row locks and block other queries. Age > 10s is worth investigating.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  trx_id,
  trx_state,
  trx_started,
  TIMESTAMPDIFF(SECOND, trx_started, NOW())  AS age_sec,
  trx_rows_locked,
  trx_rows_modified,
  trx_mysql_thread_id                        AS thread_id,
  LEFT(IFNULL(trx_query, '(no query)'), 200) AS current_query
FROM information_schema.innodb_trx
ORDER BY trx_started ASC

panels.title: InnoDB Lock Waits
panels.description: Who is blocking who right now. Empty = no contention. Kill the blocking_thread to unblock.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  r.ENGINE_TRANSACTION_ID                  AS waiting_txn,
  r.THREAD_ID                              AS waiting_thread,
  b.ENGINE_TRANSACTION_ID                  AS blocking_txn,
  b.THREAD_ID                              AS blocking_thread,
  r.OBJECT_SCHEMA                          AS db,
  r.OBJECT_NAME                            AS tbl,
  r.LOCK_TYPE                              AS lock_type,
  r.LOCK_MODE                              AS lock_mode
FROM performance_schema.data_lock_waits w
JOIN performance_schema.data_locks r
  ON r.ENGINE_LOCK_ID = w.REQUESTING_ENGINE_LOCK_ID
JOIN performance_schema.data_locks b
  ON b.ENGINE_LOCK_ID = w.BLOCKING_ENGINE_LOCK_ID

panels.title: Recent Errors — events_statements_summary_by_digest (errors > 0)
panels.description: Query patterns that produced errors. These often surface silent application bugs.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  SCHEMA_NAME                                           AS db,
  SUBSTRING(REPLACE(DIGEST_TEXT, '\n', ' '), 1, 120)  AS query_pattern,
  COUNT_STAR                                            AS calls,
  SUM_ERRORS                                            AS errors,
  SUM_WARNINGS                                          AS warnings,
  ROUND(SUM_ERRORS / COUNT_STAR * 100, 2)              AS error_rate_pct,
  LAST_SEEN                                             AS last_seen
FROM performance_schema.events_statements_summary_by_digest
WHERE SUM_ERRORS > 0
  AND SCHEMA_NAME NOT IN ('mysql', 'performance_schema', 'information_schema', 'sys')
ORDER BY SUM_ERRORS DESC
LIMIT 20

panels.title: Replication Status — all replicas
panels.description: IO thread, SQL thread, and lag per replica. Run against each replica datasource in real use.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  @@hostname                   AS replica,
  SERVICE_STATE                AS io_thread,
  LAST_ERROR_NUMBER            AS io_error_code,
  LEFT(LAST_ERROR_MESSAGE, 80) AS io_error,
  SOURCE_HOST                  AS source_host,
  SOURCE_LOG_FILE              AS log_file,
  READ_SOURCE_LOG_POS          AS log_pos
FROM performance_schema.replication_connection_status
LIMIT 1
